Name: Tampa Bay Product Group
Description:
This group is for professionals who are passionate about Agile, Product Development, and Product Creation. We connect to share experiences, knowledge, and best practices in Lean Product Development. Whether you’re a seasoned professional or just starting out, this group is the perfect place to network, learn, and grow in the field of product development. Let’s collaborate, innovate, and elevate our skills together!

Join us on Meetup: https://www.meetup.com/tampabayproductgroup/

Name: Kuwait Accountants and Auditors Association
Description:
An organization for accountancy professionals, was founded in 1973 by the Decree Law No. 5 of 1981 on the Practice of the Auditing Profession. KAAA is directly responsible for:

• Delivering initial and continuing professional development under the supervision of the MCI for members which includes Chartered Accountants and Auditors.

• Managing and conducting professional examinations with the exam committee for the certification of accountants as well as auditors.

• Maintaining a registry of its members. In addition to being a member of IFAC, KAAA is also a member of the Accounting and Auditing Organization for Islamic Financial Institutions (AAAOFI).

Name: Criminal Law Solicitors Association
Description:
The Criminal Law Solicitors Association is the only organisation entirely committed to professionals working in the field of criminal law in England and Wales.

We need you to join us so that we can represent your interests and speak on behalf of criminal law practitioners.

Membership is open to any solicitor - prosecution or defence - and to court clerks, qualified or trainee, involved with or interested in the practice of criminal law.

Benefits of joining include:
- Representation when times are rough
- Court ID Cards
- Police Station Representative Cards
- Consultations
- Influencing decision makers
- Building a strong network of criminal lawyers
- Raising your profile
- Be part of a vibrant e-group
- Regular alerts and communications
- An annaul 'not to be missed' conference
- Discount on law books
- Access to members database

Memberships are available for individuals and firms.

Name: Beyond Global
Description:
Beyond is a curated community for women across cultures, careers, geographies, and generations, providing them with inspiring, nourishing, and transformative experiences in trusted safe spaces. 

Global, multi-faceted, passionate women who embrace diversity, embody authenticity, and pursue continuous growth and impact.

Beyond creates exclusive, transformative, and cutting-edge experiences that bring women together to connect, nourish, grow, and leave a legacy.

Name: Institut PIFE
Description:
Espace dédié aux professionnels psychosociaux et juridiques œuvrant en contexte de séparation conflictuelle. 

L’Institut PIFE s’engage à former, certifier et mobiliser les professionnels psychosociaux et juridiques afin de renforcer leur capacité d’intervention dans les situations de séparation hautement conflictuelle. Notre mission est de favoriser le rétablissement du lien parent-enfant tout en cultivant une posture préventive et collaborative, permettant de préserver ce lien tout au long des trajectoires familiales post-séparation.

Nous offrons des formations rigoureuses reconnues par l’Ordre des psychologues du Québec (OPQ) et admissibles à la reconnaissance par d’autres ordres professionnels psychosociaux ainsi que par le Barreau du Québec. Nous proposons également la seule formation officielle menant à la certification en intervention PIFE, une approche familiale systémique interdisciplinaire fondée sur un protocole éprouvé. En parallèle, nous créons une synergie entre professionnels en favorisant la collaboration, l’échange de pratiques et la référence vers des intervenants certifiés.

Notre vision est collective : protéger, soutenir et préserver le développement des enfants exposés à des conflits parentaux post-séparation, en mobilisant une expertise interdisciplinaire orientée vers le rétablissement du lien parent-enfant. Cette vision repose sur une compréhension partagée du continuum de détérioration des liens familiaux post-séparation, permettant aux professionnels psychosocio-juridiques d’agir ensemble, de manière précoce et avec rigueur.

Name: Israel CoS Forum
Description:
Israel Chief of Staff Forum is the professional community for Israel’s leading Chiefs of Staff across various sectors.
The forum is dedicated to providing our members with the knowledge, tools and skills needed to succeed and grow as Chiefs of Staff and professional leaders.

If you are currently acting as a Chief of Staff, you can drop a note to join our private group:

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/groups/9068777/

Name: FPA of Metro New York
Description:
The FPA of Metro New York is a professional organization of more than 500 financial planning professionals across the metro New York City (and surrounding) area. Visit our Web site at www.fpametrony.org/

FPA is the principal professional organization for CFP professionals, educators, financial services professionals and students who seek advancement in a growing, dynamic profession. We believe true financial planners provide an invaluable, critical service to humanity, and that CFP professionals lead the way in the commitment to raise the bar for the clients they serve.

Simply put, we are the community that advances the practice and profession of financial planning. Our content, tools and resources are what make FPA membership valuable, and our passionate, thriving community of financial planners working together to lift each other -- and the profession -- to a new level, makes your FPA membership indispensable.

Learn more about FPA at OneFPA.org.

Name: CISAO - Chambre de Commerce Italie-Sénégal et Afrique de l’Ouest
Description: page officielle de la CISAO

Name: Alpha Kappa Psi - Beta Mu Chapter
Description:
The Beta Mu Chapter of Alpha Kappa Psi is one of the George Washington University's premier student organizations. Rechartered in 2008, the chapter strives to achieve every goal by living its tagline to the fullest: 'Shaping People, Shaping Business'. Currently, the chapter consists of 80-100 members with diverse backgrounds and interests in fields such as Consulting, Finance, Accounting, Medicine, Entertainment, Data Analytics, Law, International Affairs, Political Science, Communications, Economics, and much more. The chapter has over 200+ active alumni based all over the world.

The chapter's keystone initiatives include an annual career conference (Ready, Set, Grow), D.C. & New York Treks, Investment Committee, and Philanthropy Week. Other programming includes Technical Workshops (Excel, Python, Tableau), Alumni Receptions, and a professional development Mentorship Program. 

Recruitment occurs at the beginning of every Fall and Spring semester. Alpha Kappa Psi is a Coed Business Fraternity open to all majors.

Contact president.betamu@gmail.com for any inquiries.

Name: Youth awake Togo
Description:
YOUTH AWAKE est une organisation à but non lucratif créée par des jeunes promoteurs de développement dont l’objectif est de construire une communauté de jeunes capables de prévenir et de gérer les conflits sociaux, garantir la paix et accélérer le processus d’intégration des jeunes pour un développement socioéconomique durable.

Créée en 2016 et comptant quatorze (14) personnes au départ, cette organisation compte aujourd’hui plus de 1 000 jeunes membres sur le plan national qui engagés en plein pieds pour l’atteinte de ses objectifs.

Name: RIAFPI - Réseau International des Agences Francophones de Promotion des Investissements
Description:
Le 17 mars 2014 à Abidjan est créé le Réseau International des Agences Francophones de Promotion des Investissements (RIAFPI). 

L’idée de créer le RIAFPI est née de la nécessité :
- d’établir une chaîne de solidarité entre les APIs qui ont en commun le Français comme langue de travail ;
- de créer une plateforme de concertation entre les pays francophones en tenant compte des problèmes liés à leur développement respectif ;
- d’échanger les expériences en vue d’accroître la capacité d’attraction des IDE en faveur d’une plus forte implication des pays francophones dans l’économie mondiale.

Le RIAFPI compte à ce jour 20 APIs : Bénin, Burkina Faso, Burundi, Cameroun, Comores, Côte d’Ivoire, France, Gabon, Guinée, Guinée-Bissau, Liban, Mali, Maroc, Niger, République Démocratique du Congo, République du Congo Brazzaville, Sénégal, Tchad, Ile Maurice et Haïti.

Le réseau bénéficie du concours technique d’Organisations multilatérales que sont la Conférence des Nations Unies sur le Commerce et le Développement (CNUCED), la Banque Africaine de Développement (BAD), l’Organisation des Nations Unies pour le Développement Industriel (ONUDI) et l’Organisation Internationale de la Francophonie (OIF) qui elle siège en qualité de membre observateur du réseau.

Name: TOP 100 CEOs

We organize award ceremonies that bring under one roof powerful and influential men and women leading various organizations.

La GEFEL s’implique sur de nombreux sujets allant de la production à la commercialisation, en passant par les sujets de logistique, de financements publics.

La GEFEL siège au Conseil d'Administration de l'interprofession des fruits et légumes (INTERFEL), et dans ses groupes de travail. Elle y assure le dialogue avec les acteurs de la filière, amont et aval.
La GEFEL siège également au ministère de l'Agriculture, que ce soit en Commission Nationale Technique, au Conseil Spécialisé F&L de FranceAgriMer, et au sein de Groupe de travail sur les Programmes Opérationnels. 

Dans son groupe d’experts dédié, la GEFEL associe en effet 11 de la quinzaine de spécialistes français sur les OP/ AOP et Programmes Opérationnels, une force vive qui lui permet de participer activement à tous les Groupes de Travail et consultation de l’Administration sur les PO.

Wir verstehen uns daher auch als eine Anlaufstelle für Mitglieder und Außenstehende, die Rat und Hilfe im deutsch-indischen Rechtsverkehr suchen.

We are committed, both at the national and European level, to improving the legal framework for German-Indian business relations.

The DIJV aims to build bridges between Germany and India and to establish and maintain relevant contacts. This applies both within the DIJV and in the public sphere.

We therefore also see ourselves as a point of contact for members and outsiders seeking advice and assistance in Indo-German legal matters.

Name: Malta Chamber of Geologists  (MCG)
Description:
The Malta Chamber of Geologists (MCG) is a non-profit registered organisation (VO/2329) established to represent and defend the Geological profession in Malta and to promote and distribute research and knowledge in the fields of Geology and Earth Sciences in Malta. The management of the MCG is undertaken by the Executive Council. The MCG was set up in the interests of public safety and to promote high standards in Geology and its applications. 

- The MCG contributes to the protection of the environment, public safety and responsible exploitation of natural resources and promotes excellence in the application of geology. 

- The MCG promotes common European policy on matters such as geology in planning, renewable energies, monitoring and containing natural hazards and, economic and sustainable use of national mineral resources. 

- The MCG promotes education and awareness for citizens at all levels on the importance of Geology for environmental protection, sustainable development and public safety.

Name: First Generation Professionals of Milwaukee
Description:

Description: The APTA Academy of Research, a component of the American Physical Therapy Association, is dedicated to advancing the field of physical therapy through innovative research, education, and collaboration. We focus on enhancing the roles of physical therapists, physical therapist assistants, and students as leaders in clinical, academic, and community settings. Our mission is to promote excellence in research and evidence-based practice to improve patient outcomes and community health. Name: Norwegian Water Cluster - Vannklyngen
Description:
Vannklyngen – Norwegian Water Cluster er en medlemsorganisasjon med over 50 medlemsbedrifter innen vannbransjen. Vannklyngens formål er å samle og fronte norsk vannbransje, slik at vi i fellesskap kan løse utfordringene norsk vannbransje står overfor. Ved å bygge opp et faglig sterkt og kompetent nettverk, skal Vannklyngen også nå ut i det globale vannmarkedet og være en bidragsyter til at verden når FNs bærekraftsmål for Vann innen 2030. 
Internasjonalt skal Vannklyngen skal være portåpneren inn til norsk vannteknologi. Vi skal inspirere, oppmuntre og tilrettelegge for fremtidsrettede teknologier ved å koble sammen nøkkelaktørenes erfaringer, kompetanse og lederskap.  
På det nasjonale plan vil Vannklyngen arbeide for å være den fasilitatoren som bringer alle aktører sammen, få vann inn på den politiske agendaen, og oppfordre kommuner til å prioritere vann og informere folk om vann og vannforvaltning generelt. 
Vannklyngen finansieres gjennom medlemsinntekter og med støtte fra Vestfold og Telemark Fylkeskommune. 
Har du et problem eller utfordring som trenger ekspertisen til en eller flere Vannklyngebedrifter hjelper vi mer enn gjerne med kontaktformidling og kan også sette opp workshops for å utvikle eller utrede problemstillinger for å kommer frem til gode løsninger.

We host events, workshops, and networking opportunities centered around the latest in go-to-market execution, demand generation, product marketing, and more. Après avoir été pionniers sur l’accès des cabinets au marché des particuliers ECF Services poursuit l’objectif de développer le service apporté à ses coopérateurs  en enrichissant son offre avec un accès exclusif à de la documentation professionnelle consacrée à la gestion de patrimoine et des offres partenaires à tarifs ultra préférentiels.

Name: Syndicat National du Théâtre Privé (SNDTP)
Description:
Clef de voûte de la profession, le Syndicat National du Théâtre Privé (SNDTP) est l’une des organisations patronales incontournable dans la branche du spectacle vivant privé.

Depuis 2016 le SNDTP a fondé SCÈNES, la Fédération de la Création Artistique Privée, avec CAMULC, Syndicat National des Cabarets, Music-halls & Lieux de Création, qui est désormais la seconde organisation patronale représentative de la branche du spectacle vivant privé (IDCC3090).

En lien avec les pouvoirs publics, les parlementaires mais aussi les représentants des collectivités locales, le SNDTP entend défendre la création théâtrale indépendante dans toute sa diversité.

Le SNDTP négocie pour le compte de ses adhérents des accords particuliers avec les acteurs essentiels du secteur (SACD, SACEM, SCPP, SPPF, Audiens Prévoyance, AFDAS…) afin d’aménager des dispositifs spécifiques aux bénéfices de ces derniers. 

Il développe en outre des actions collectives de communication visant à promouvoir les entreprises de théâtre et leurs spectacles. 

L’équipe permanente du Syndicat National du Théâtre Privé ainsi que ses conseils assurent à ses adhérents des services individualisés et collectifs en matière juridique, social et fiscal (séminaires d’information, groupes de travail techniques, assistances téléphoniques, etc.).

Name: Society for Ear, Nose & Throat Advances in Children (SENTAC)
Description:
The Society for Ear, Nose & Throat Advances in Children (SENTAC) is a multidisciplinary professional organization dedicated to advancing the care of children with ear, nose, and throat (ENT) conditions through collaboration, education, research, and advocacy.

SENTAC brings together physicians, audiologists, speech-language pathologists, advanced practice providers, researchers, trainees, and more professionals who share a commitment to improving outcomes for pediatric patients and their families. By fostering meaningful interdisciplinary engagement, SENTAC serves as a platform for innovation, knowledge exchange, and professional growth across the full spectrum of pediatric ENT care.

What We Do:
-Annual Scientific Meeting: A highly regarded, multidisciplinary conference featuring cutting-edge research, clinical advances, and collaborative dialogue across professions
-Education & Professional Development: Opportunities for lifelong learning, mentorship, and leadership development for clinicians at all career stages
-Research & Scholarship: Support for dissemination of pediatric ENT research, including presentations, publications, and research recognition
-Community & Collaboration: A growing network that values inclusion, interdisciplinary respect, and shared problem-solving
-Advocacy & Awareness: Efforts to elevate pediatric ENT issues and improve care delivery at local, regional, and national levels

SENTAC prides itself on being a welcoming, inclusive organization where collaboration across disciplines is not just encouraged—it is foundational. Members are united by a shared goal: to improve the health, development, and quality of life of children with ENT-related conditions.

Whether you are a seasoned clinician, researcher, educator, or trainee, SENTAC offers a professional home where your work, ideas, and contributions can make a meaningful impact.

Together, we are advancing pediatric ENT care—today and for the future.

Name: OndeVivo Associação

Com uma abordagem prática, ética e de proximidade, atuamos em escolas, autarquias e territórios vulneráveis, capacitando pessoas e empresários para gerirem melhor o seu dinheiro, compreenderem os seus direitos fiscais e navegarem com segurança no mundo digital.

Na OndeVivo Associação, acreditamos que compreender é o primeiro passo para transformar.

Promovemos uma aprendizagem ativa e capacitação comunitária através de workshops, sessões de esclarecimento e ações sociais focadas na literacia fiscal, económico-financeira e digital. Atuamos com diferentes públicos (com alunos nas escolas, adultos e empresários), contribuindo para a construção de uma cidadania informada, autónoma e socialmente responsável.

Investir na educação financeira desde cedo é garantir um futuro mais estável para todos. Acreditamos que o conhecimento individual é uma semente para o crescimento da comunidade.

Name: TRIIBE
Description:
We work with nonprofit founders in college where vision is high but support is scarce. Across our cohorts, founders collectively represent 61 years of nonprofit leadership, each having run their organizations for an average of six years in one of these six sectors: food, water, shelter, health, education, and energy.

Today, over 4,000 student-led nonprofits exist in the U.S., but 80% fade within a year. Once in college, founders face a sharp funding gap compared to high school, along with the disconnection that comes with resetting in a new environment. With less than one percent of funding reaching youth-led nonprofits, sustaining progress becomes increasingly difficult, especially with looming financial pressures after graduation and a spiking youth unemployment rate.  

We are building the college pipeline, like finance or consulting, but for social impact. TRIIBE is a three-year fellowship for cohorts of five nonprofit founders looking to further their nonprofits even after graduation. Onboarding a new cohort each semester, we provide: 

Funding: Three scaling grants ($5K sophomore year, $10K junior year, $25K senior year), curated grant leads, and donor visibility from website, podcast, workshop, and media features.

Mentorship: Financial and legal counsel, plus two personalized mentors each quarter (two dozen by graduation) matched for industry, passion, and strategic fit.

Community: Changemaker Summit, retreat, and monthly sessions connecting founders with peers, alumni, and supporters. Having already partnered with over 2 dozen companies, we ensure our founders are supported on all fronts.

Real change cannot be made alone, it takes a TRIIBE. 

The centerpiece of our community is the Changemaker Summit, which has already convened hundreds of executives, founders, and investors to share insights and spark collaborations. Next-gen changemakers are the focus, not the footnote, with our cohorts taking center stage on panels and in discussions.

Name: Colegio Oficial de Médicos de Salamanca
Description:
El Colegio Oficial de Médicos de Salamanca integrado en la Organización Médica Colegial, es una Corporación de Derecho público, amparada por la Constitución Española, por las Leyes Estatal y Autonómica de los Colegios Profesionales y por los Estatutos Generales de la Organización Médica Colegial, con estructuras democráticamente construidas, carácter representativo, personalidad jurídica propia y capacidad para el cumplimiento de sus fines, independiente de las Administraciones Públicas, sin perjuicio de las relaciones de Derecho público que con ellas legalmente le corresponda.
Corresponde al Colegio la representación exclusiva de la profesión médica, la ordenación en el ámbito territorial de su competencia de la actividad profesional de los Colegiados y la defensa de sus intereses profesionales.

Name: Join The Planet
Description:
We create valuable products with iconic public figures, developed under conscious and transparent value chains.


Our mission is to foster collaborative projects among artists, celebrities, and the community to craft marketable products and allocate profits to environmental causes.

We create unique products that raise awareness and raise funds for environmental causes. Our value lies in our ability to bring people from around the world to protect and preserve our natural environment. From designing sustainable products to collaborating with leading environmental organizations, every step we take is a step towards a brighter, greener future.

Name: Organización Panameña de Comunidades de Tecnología y Comunicación (OPACTIC)
Description:
La Organización Panameña de Comunidades de Tecnologías de Información y Comunicaciones (OPACTIC) es una comunidad de profesionales, estudiantes y entusiastas del área de IT. 

Tenemos como objetivo coordinar e informar a nuestros miembros acerca de eventos, seminarios presenciales, webinars, entrenamientos, concursos, licitaciones, certificaciones, oportunidades de trabajo en cualquier posición de tecnología y demás; buscando siempre compartir información que sea provechosa para nuestra comunidad.

Te invitamos a formar parte, siguiendo el enlace en nuestro perfil.

Name: TwynstraGudde | Managers van Morgen
Description:
In ons werk staan grote maatschappelijke vraagstukken centraal, waar vaak extra daadkracht nodig is. Of het nu gaat om het aanpakken van personeelstekorten in het onderwijs of het mogelijk maken van waardig herstel van gedupeerde ouders van de Toeslagenaffaire: met onze inzet dragen wij bij aan een publieke sector waarin organisaties klaar zijn voor morgen en waar burgers zich gezien en gesteund voelen.  

Dit doen wij omdat we geloven in een toekomst waarin het openbaar bestuur slagvaardig opereert en in staat is mee te bewegen met grote maatschappelijke uitdagingen. Wij zien een toekomst voor ons waar iedere burger gezien wordt, omdat de menselijke maat centraal staat en transparantie en verantwoordelijkheid vanzelfsprekend zijn. Wij zien een toekomst voor ons waar vitale maatschappelijke voorzieningen zoals onderwijs en zorg weer floreren, omdat deze sectoren wendbaar en weerbaar zijn. Een toekomst waarin we uitgaan van vertrouwen en werken aan het creëren van gelijke kansen. En waar we door digitalisering een inclusievere en voor iedereen toegankelijke dienstverlening bieden. En, een toekomst waarin duurzaamheid verankerd is in keuzes van vandaag, zodat we recht doen aan de generaties van morgen.   

Voor ons begint die toekomst in het hier en nu. 

Wij geloven dat deze uitdagingen nu om nieuw leiderschap vragen. Hiervoor is een nieuwe generatie leiders nodig. Leiders met lef, die patronen durven te doorbreken. Leiders die adaptief zijn en hierdoor ruimte maken voor nieuwe inzichten en perspectieven. ’Managers van Morgen’ levert daarom interim-managers die inspringen op actuele maatschappelijke vraagstukken in de publieke sector. Of het nu gaat om het versnellen van verandering of het creëren van rust in turbulente omgevingen: wij staan klaar om impact te maken op de uitdagingen van vandaag én morgen.

Name: Femtech University Network (FUN)
Description:
We promote education, innovation, investment, and research in technology addressing that addresses unmet needs in women’s health by facilitating inter-university collaboration. 

Broadly defined as the intersection of women’s health and technology, femtech encompasses all innovative solutions targeting unmet needs in diseases, conditions and indications that solely, predominantly, or differently impact the health of women. Importantly, this goes beyond fertility and menstrual health to common diseases that affect women and people of other genders differently, such as brain health, cardiovascular diseases, endocrinology, autoimmune diseases, and many more.

Name: CalCPA Sacramento Chapter
Description:
Welcome to the Sacramento Chapter of CalCPA, your local hub for connecting with finance and accounting professionals. We're not just about promoting business growth and upholding integrity, we're about fostering relationships and creating a strong local community of accounting professionals. Because the future of accounting is anything, but by the numbers, we aim to redefine the image of the profession while enhancing the career experience of our members.

In our community, you have the opportunity to connect, collaborate and grow with local professionals who are driving transformation in the field. Join our chapter today, connect with local leaders and be part of a community shaping the future.

Name: CarolinaCISO
Description:
CarolinaCISO is the preeminent peer leadership network of Carolina chief information security officers. Our membership is comprised exclusively of CISOs (or equivalent executive roles) from public and private companies, government, education, healthcare and nonprofit organizations.

Our mission is to build a strong, professional community among Carolina's information technology leaders, by providing a forum to share ideas, best practices and experiences relevant to CISOs.

We create opportunities for technology leaders to engage in substantive, issues-based discussion related to their industries and profession.  Members benefit by collaborating with others, building meaningful relationships, and promoting professional development through executive events that facilitate valuable interaction among senior-level experts.

MEMBERSHIP QUALIFICATION 
Members must be a Carolina CISO or equivalent senior technology leader for an organization with at least $100 million annual revenue. Organizations over $1 billion qualify for a second membership and organizations over $5 billion may enroll a third member – provided the organization’s primary technology leader is a member in good standing.

ABOUT THE ORBIE AWARDS 
Established in 1998, the ORBIE Awards is the premier technology executive recognition program in the United States. This annual event honors chief information security officers who have demonstrated excellence in technology leadership.

Name: AAREP Chicago - formerly Chi-AACRE
Description:
African American Real Estate Professionals – Chicago (AAREP Chicago) is part of a national network that empowers Black professionals in commercial real estate. Our mission is to support, advance and promote the intellectual, professional and economic development of African Americans in and associated with the commercial real estate industry.

AAREP Chicago supports and connects a wide range of CRE industry professionals—including developers, brokers, investment and asset/property managers, architects, engineers, contractors, attorneys, government officials, educators, capital markets/finance and tax experts – by fostering relationships, sharing resources, and creating pathways for both current success and future growth.

AAREP Chicago is committed to cultivating a strong network of thought leaders and advocates who are deeply invested in the city’s future.

Name: Sankore
Description:
Sankore is a boutique strategy and transformation firm that sits at the intersection of leadership, technology, and justice-centered change. We partner with organizations that are navigating complex digital shifts : AI adoption, new operating models, culture change, and need more than another toolkit or one-off training.

We help leadership teams design and implement transformation they can actually sustain. That means aligning strategy, structure, and culture so technology investments translate into real outcomes for people and communities.

What we do
AI and digital transformation strategy rooted in human dynamics
Leadership and team development for courageous, future-ready decision making
Facilitation of strategic planning, change initiatives, and cross-functional alignment
Design and delivery of action-learning labs, workshops, and leadership cohorts

How we work
Our approach is highly interactive, framework-driven, and unapologetically practical. We don’t drop in with pre-packaged answers. We co-create operating models, decision frameworks, and action roadmaps that leaders can use long after the engagement ends.

Sankore is the organizational transformation arm of the broader Sankore | Lift.ED ecosystem.
 Through Sankore, we focus on institutions, networks, and enterprises; through Lift.ED, we support education systems and learning organizations.

If your organization is ready to move from talking about change to executing it, Sankore helps you build the leadership capacity and digital strategy to get there.

Name: The Institute of Internal Auditors Detroit Chapter
Description:
The Detroit Chapter of the IIA serves the internal audit community of the Metro-Detroit area of Southeast Michigan.

Established in 1941, The Institute of Internal Auditors (IIA) is an international professional association with global headquarters in Lake Mary, Florida, USA. The IIA is the internal audit profession's global voice, recognized authority, acknowledged leader, chief advocate, and principal educator. Generally, members work in internal auditing, risk management, governance, internal control, information technology audit, education, and security.

Globally, The IIA has more than 200,000 members. The IIA in North America comprises 159 chapters serving more than 70,000 members in the United States, Canada, the Caribbean (Aruba, Bahamas, Barbados, Cayman Islands, Curacao, Jamaica, Puerto Rico, and Turks & Caicos), Bermuda, Guyana, and Trinidad & Tobago. Members enjoy benefits offered by the North American Service Center including local, national, and global professional networking; world-class training; certification; standards and guidance; research; executive development; career opportunities; and more. Internal auditors throughout North America enjoy free members-only webinars and national conferences such as the General Audit Management Conference, the Governance, Risk, and Control (GRC) Conference, and the All Star Conference. The IIA’s Audit Executive Center provides chief audit executives relevant and timely thought leadership and connections to peers for benchmarking and sharing best practices. And resources such as IIA Quality Services make The Institute an indispensable partner to you and your organization.

Name: Krajowa Izba Managerów Opieki Senioralnej
Description:
To pierwsza ogólnopolska Inicjatywa służąca szeroko pojętej Integracji środowisk zawodowo związanych z holistycznie pojmowaną opieką - zarówno zdrowotną, jak i w obszarze pomocy społecznej, świadczoną w instytucjach oraz w warunkach domowych. Inspiracją do powołania tego typu organizacji, stała się stale podkreślana potrzeba wypracowania modelowego systemu koordynowanej opieki zdrowotnej, połączonej ze wsparciem z obszaru szeroko rozumianej pomocy społecznej kierowanej do osób niesamodzielnych lub o ograniczonej samodzielności, ze szczególnym uwzględnieniem osób starszych (seniorów).

Pragniemy, aby Izba stała się swoistą "strefą spotkań" - służącą wymianie doświadczeń, dążących do wypracowania optymalnych standardów oraz dobrych praktyk w obszarze szeroko pojmowanej opieki nad osobami obarczonymi ograniczeniami w samodzielności, ze szczególnym uwzględnieniem potrzeb osób starszych (seniorów). Współpraca oraz członkostwo w Izbie zapewni Państwu dostęp do profesjonalnie przygotowanych form wsparcia i pomocy, gwarantujących komfort i poczucie bezpieczeństwa w trakcie realizacji zadań opiekuńczych. 

Do członkostwa w Izbie zapraszamy wszystkie osoby i instytucje oraz podmioty realizujące swoją aktywność zawodową w obszarze holistycznej opieki zdrowotnej oraz pomocy społecznej.

Name: Loyola Chicago Business Law Society
Description: The mission of the Business Law Society (BLS) shall be to promote social and academic interaction among Loyola students interested in the intersection of business and the law. The Organization will be open to students interested in various aspects of transactional and litigation-based business law. The BLS will sponsor social activities, speakers, and events that foster academic discussion and provide a forum through which members may pursue and develop their passion for a career in business law. The goal of this Organization will be to enhance the legal education of our members and to promote an inter-disciplinary curriculum in business, corporate, and financial law that utilizes the resources of Loyola University Chicago School of Law.

The American Entomological Society currently publishes two journals, Transactions of the American Entomological Society and Entomological News.

Name: Mehran University Mathematics Society
Description:
The Mehran University Mathematics Society (MUMS) is a newly established organization with a mission to provide a platform for students to showcase their talents and passion for mathematics. MUMS aims to create a community of like-minded individuals who can come together to share their knowledge, collaborate on projects, and participate in various mathematics-related activities.
MUMS hosts a wide range of events, including workshops, seminars, and competitions, designed to challenge and inspire students to explore the field of mathematics and also provides opportunities for students to present their own research and projects, giving them a platform to showcase their talents and gain valuable experience. 
In addition to fostering a love for mathematics, MUMS also encourages students to develop important skills such as problem-solving, critical thinking, and teamwork, which are essential for success in any field. With a supportive community and an array of exciting opportunities, MUMS is open to all students who are interested in mathematics, regardless of their major or level of expertise.

Name: Arizona Women Leading Government (AZ WLG)
Description:
Arizona Women Leading Government (AZ WLG) is a non-profit membership association that provides professional development, networking, and mentoring opportunities statewide to strengthen the position of women by preparing aspiring, emerging professionals for leadership posts in government; and to increase the number of women in executive government positions throughout the State of Arizona. 
 
Arizona Women Leading Government (AZ WLG) is the state chapter of Women Leading Government (WLG) which is part of the International City/County Management Association (ICMA) #SheLeadsGov Network. AZ WLG was formed in 2015 in partnership with the Arizona City/County Management Association (ACMA), incorporated in 2022 with a Board of Directors and bylaws, and is staffed by the League of Arizona Cities and Towns. 
 
AZ WLG Members are all people dedicated to helping women leaders to succeed in public service. AZ WLG holds an annual conference which in 2023 drew more than 500 government professionals and other attendees who support our mission.  
 
For more information about Arizona Women Leading Government (AZ WLG) please visit ArizonaWLG.org.

Name: Indonesia Zero Emission Association (AZEI)
Description:
AZEI – Asosiasi Zero Emisi Indonesia
Indonesia’s independent association for zero-emissions leadership — convening standards, policy, and inclusive participation.

AZEI is an independent national platform accelerating Indonesia’s net-zero transition that is inclusive, credible, and aligned with national priorities. We convene state, industry, academia, and communities so standards, markets, and policy move together — pro-growth, pro-investment, and pro-people.

Founded by leaders across parliament, academia, and industry, with a multi-stakeholder council, AZEI pairs national legitimacy with independent voice. Our role is to strengthen state and private efforts with a neutral forum to align priorities, clarify rules, and expand participation. We safeguard integrity, widen access, and build structures that endure.

Focus areas
• Policy & standards — Align national rules with VCM, JCM, and Article 6 to reinforce credibility.
• Inclusion & access — Via Carbon Justice Indonesia, enable fair, verifiable participation and tangible local benefits.
• MRV & registry — Promote streamlined, auditable digital MRV built to integrate with the national registry.
• Public–private bridge — Host executive forums that remove investment barriers and produce outcomes.
• Capacity & talent — Develop training, certification, and playbooks for government, industry, and developers.
• International collaboration — Build partnerships that strengthen Indonesia’s global role and expand market access.

Why partner with AZEI?
	1.	Legitimacy & access — Direct channels to policymakers and industry leaders.
	2.	Acceleration — Networks and frameworks that cut friction and move policy into practice.
	3.	Measurable impact — Participation-based KPIs and auditable co-benefits that build trust.

Indonesia’s path to net zero must be structured, inclusive, and sovereign. AZEI exists to embed those principles so the transition delivers prosperity at home and global recognition.

Name: Conseil national de la Jeunesse du Niger
Description:
Le Conseil National de la Jeunesse du Niger (CNJ-Niger) est l’organe représentatif de la jeunesse nigérienne. Il œuvre pour la participation active des jeunes au développement national, la promotion du leadership, la citoyenneté, la paix et l’emploi des jeunes.

À travers ses programmes, formations et partenariats, le CNJ-Niger agit pour renforcer la voix des jeunes dans la gouvernance, l’innovation sociale et le développement durable.

Vision : Une jeunesse consciente, responsable et actrice du changement.
Mission : Représenter, défendre et promouvoir les intérêts des jeunes du Niger dans tous les domaines.
Valeurs : Engagement – Solidarité – Citoyenneté – Responsabilité.

Name: Normandie, Terre d'Appellations Cidricoles
Description:
Créé en 1975, le CICD,  au départ comité interprofessionnel des commissions de dégustation, puis, depuis 2008, comité de soutien aux ODG cidricoles de Normandie et du Maine (association loi 1901), devient Normandie, Terre d'Appellations Cidricoles (NTAC). Normandie, Terre d'Appellations Cidricoles est reconnu Organisme de Défense et de Gestion (ODG), pour les 8 appellations d'origine contrôlées et protégées cidricoles normandes.  
Ses adhérents sont les opérateurs de chacune de ces 8 appellations d'origine : Calvados, Calvados Pays d'Auge, Calvados Domfrontais, Pommeau de Normandie, cidre Pays d'Auge, cidre Cotentin, cidre du Perche, poiré Domfront. Ainsi, Normandie, Terre d'Appellations Cidricoles regroupe 3300 adhérents, les producteurs de fruits, transformateurs, distillateurs, chais de vieillissement… 
Les missions de Normandie, Terre d'Appellations Cidricoles sont celles d'un ODG et définies dans le code rural. Elles recouvrent la gestion de chaque appellation et la valorisation et la protection des terroirs et des savoir-faire associés à ces appellations.

Name: Bulgarian Industrial Capital Association
Description:
Founded in 1996, Bulgarian Industrial Capital Association (BICA) is the most extensively represented organization of Bulgarian employers. It is also the most dynamically developing organization of Bulgarian businesses, which is represented at national level.
Members of the Association are over 100 branch chambers, representing more than 75% of the economic activities in this country, and over 10 000 enterprises that employ almost 500 000 people. BICA has a network of regional chambers in 2/3 of the municipalities in the country.
Bulgarian Industrial Capital Association has earned itself the reputation of a speaker of the actual manufacturing sector. It protects the interests of its members in front of the executive and legislative authorities at national and European level, influences the preparation of the normative base and the policies in the economic sector, makes systematic efforts for improving the competitiveness of Bulgarian enterprises and for creating favorable business environment and for enhancing the Bulgarian economy in general. Throughout the years the Association has implemented consistent policies and has made systematic actions; it has built on the achieved success and has been equally unbiased and independent of all political parties.
Bulgarian Industrial Capital Association is the only organization that incorporates public companies and providers of services of general interests in the country. The Association is permanently committed to the capital markets development and makes constant efforts for the positive perception of issuers’ statute. The Association works in this direction in close collaboration with the investment community and with institutions at national and European level.
BICA is the only Bulgarian nationally representative organization of employers that is member of SGI Europe – European Centre of Employers and Enterprises providing Public services (www.sgieurope.org).

Name: Association of Black Anthropologists
Description:
The Association for Black Anthropologists (ABA) was founded in 1970 by a small group of intellectuals who sought to break down barriers that impeded their full participation in the discipline of Anthropology. By creating scholarship that linked anthropological theory to struggles for social justice, these elders transformed anthropology and helped create generations of intellectual leaders.

ABA brings together Black Anthropologists and other scholars who are concerned with the goals of the ABA and who support its activities.

ABA seeks to ensure that people studied by anthropologists are not only objects of study but active makers and/or participants in their own history. In a larger sense, we intend to highlight situations of exploitation, oppression and discrimination.

Further it is our objective to analyze and critique social science theories that misrepresent the reality of exploited groups while at the same time construct more adequate theories to interpret the dynamics of oppression.

We will encourage anthropologists to involve the people being studied and the local scholars, whenever possible, in all stages of research and dissemination of findings.

We are committed to encouraging the participation of students of anthropology, recruiting Black graduate students, enrolling Black graduate students to the ABA and mentoring students involved in ABA.

Finally we will strive to establish firmer connections and scholarly exchange among Black anthropologists, especially African anthropologists.

Name: WiN Pakistan
Description:
WiN Pakistan (Women in Nuclear Pakistan) is a distinguished chapter of WiN Global, an intercultural non-governmental organization dedicated to supporting and empowering skilled women in the nuclear sector. Established in 2005 under the umbrella of the Pakistan Nuclear Society (PNS), WiN Pakistan serves as a platform to promote the peaceful uses of nuclear technology and to foster professional development in nuclear science, technology, and radiation applications.

Our Mission:
WiN Pakistan is committed to spreading awareness about the peaceful applications of nuclear technology with a special focus on women and children. By facilitating public dialogue and professional skill development, we work to empower women in nuclear science, engineering, and related fields. Our goal is to bridge generations of nuclear professionals, encouraging gender balance and diversity while creating a strong community of knowledge sharing.

As part of WiN Global, we actively contribute to the global conversations on climate change and gender balance. Through our network, we promote the exchange of knowledge and experiences between members and chapters, create partnerships, and provide development and learning opportunities to our members. Our efforts aim to spark greater interest in nuclear engineering, science, and other nuclear-related professions.

Key Objectives:
1. Promote awareness of the benefits of nuclear and radiation applications.
2. Support gender equality and diversity within the nuclear industry.
3. Foster national and international collaboration by sharing resources and knowledge.
4. Organize conferences, workshops, and meetings to exchange ideas and experiences across the nuclear community.
5. Build bridges between generations to ensure a strong, sustainable future for the nuclear industry.

We invite professionals, students, and enthusiasts in the nuclear sector to join us in our mission to create a more diverse, inclusive, and sustainable nuclear future! \(^_^)/

Name: Association of Muslim Accountants & Lawyers (AMAL)
Description:
AMAL, the Association of Muslim Accountants and Lawyers was established in 1984.


AMAL members have always held and maintained the goal of empowerment through knowledge. AMAL has held many events in aid of educating and empowering the public in terms of the accounting, commercial and legal domains and continues to strive towards this goal by hosting continuous education seminars, conferences, etc (details of these events can be enquired at the AMAL offices).

AMAL, as an organization, added a new dimension to Islamic activity in South Africa in that it brought to the fore Islamic economic and legal thought and practice, and highlighted the need for the development of this in South Africa as well as the implementation of this in daily practices. In their early years, AMAL had hosted the first ever Islamic Economics Conference in South Africa. This conference provided the profile of AMAL with a tremendous lift in its embryonic stage. The conference served as a platform to highlight some initiatives in South Africa in regard to the Islamic Economics sphere, with Professor Khurshid Ahmed as the key note speaker. Additionally, this conference had created possibilities for Islamic Banking and Finance within our country by creating a sense of consciousness towards Islamic sensitive ethics in terms of Banking and Finance.

Our association organizes courses and conferences, participates in national policy making and advisory meetings and is strongly connected with international sports medicine organizations.

Description:
At The Chick Collective, we’re redefining how women in Western Australia connect, collaborate & grow.

Born from a desire to create better, more inclusive experiences for women across all industries, our community brings together driven, generous & growth-minded women through thoughtfully curated events that spark genuine connection.

Too often, women are invited to spaces that feel ad-hoc, exclusive or inconsistent & we knew there was a better way.

So, we built it.

Our events are intentionally designed to be:

 Warm, welcoming & high-energy
 Structured, consistent & accessible
 Filled with opportunities to learn, collaborate & forge new relationships

From inspiring guest speakers to vibrant networking events, high teas, Galentine’s celebrations & boutique collaborations, every experience we create is crafted to foster real conversations, real opportunities & real community.

We believe women, teams & businesses deserve more. Events that feel thoughtfully planned, beautifully executed & fully centered around helping everyone thrive.

And that’s exactly what we deliver.

Whether you're joining us to make new connections, grow your network, showcase your business or simply be part of something uplifting ... We’d love to welcome you into our Collective.

Here, collaboration comes first.
Here, women show up as they are & are supported, celebrated & inspired.
Here, we build communities where everyone can shine brightly.

Join us. 

The Chick Collective is just getting started

La CNEMa constitue une force de proposition, de réflexion et de décision politique en matière de formation, de soins et de recherche dans le domaine de la maïeutique, auprès des instances officielles publiques, privées, professionnelles et ordinales nationales et internationales. Name: Connexion Career Collective
Description:
The Connexion Career Collective is a cross-industry, cross-generational trusted network built to accelerate personal and business growth for women and allies. Through strategic connections, commercial opportunities, and a powerful pay-it-forward culture, we unite ambitious individuals and organisations who share one goal: growth.

Founder Message
I created the Connexion Career Collective to scale what transformed my own career. My journey from Fashion to Fintech was powered by mentors, coaches, and champions. I opened doors, joined the dots, and created opportunities I couldn’t have accessed alone through my connections fuelling business visibility and growth.

Yet across Tech, Financial Services, and Professional Services, I’ve seen how many talented women and progressive organisations still face limited representation, limited access, and limited opportunity. CCC exists to change that.

We unlock commercial value by:

Expanding networks and reach

Driving visibility and influence

Nurturing talent and accelerating leadership pipelines

Strengthening high-performing teams

supporting organisational growth, retention, and innovation

CCC helps individuals and businesses rise faster, lead stronger and we do it through community, connection, and collective ambition.

Name: TriangleCIO
Description:
TriangleCIO is one of 30 chapters in the Inspire Leadership Network, a national membership organization comprised exclusively of CIOs
from public and private businesses, government, education, healthcare, and nonprofit institutions.

TriangleCIO exists to help CIOs thrive in today’s most
challenging C-suite executive role. The chapter regularly
convenes leading CIOs and fosters meaningful relationships
by hosting non-commercial, member-led programs—helping
CIOs gain leadership advantage.

We create opportunities for technology leaders to engage in substantive, issues-based discussion related to their industries and profession.  Members benefit by collaborating with others, building meaningful relationships, and promoting professional development through executive events that facilitate valuable interaction among senior-level experts.

MEMBERSHIP QUALIFICATION 
Members must be a Triangle-based CIO or equivalent senior technology leader for an organization with at least $100 million annual revenue.  Organizations over $1 billion qualify for a second membership and organizations over $5 billion may enroll a third member – provided the organization’s primary technology leader is a member in good standing.

ABOUT THE ORBIE AWARDS 
The ORBIE Awards is the premier technology executive recognition program in the United States. Since inception in 1998, over 500 technology leaders have received the prestigious ORBIE Award. The ORBIE honors chief information officers who have demonstrated excellence in technology leadership.

Our primary objectives are to:
- Provide leadership, professional unity for those in the credit management space

Description:
The Project Management Institute Wichita Chapter vision is to empower project management practitioners to achieve their full potential and success.

The PMI Wichita Chapter's mission is to provide a professional community that is mutually supportive and is a positive learning environment where every individual is given the opportunity to develop competencies that enable them to successfully manage projects.

Take advantage of the many chapter opportunities for you to:

CONNECT: Meet fellow project managers at chapter-sponsored events and monthly meetings. Build your professional network and gain insider connections at companies in your area who can help you find new opportunities.

DEVELOP: Continue to be relevant and stay up-to-date on emerging trends in the project management field.  As the project management field changes with the demand of new skills, chapters provide opportunities to upskill and advance your professional development. 

GIVE BACK: Social good activities organized by your chapter enable project managers to drive change. You can make an impact by dedicating your skills, expertise and time to help your local community.

The hub reinforces local entrepreneurship and builds bridges between local and EU innovation networks.

The EIT strengthens Europe’s ability to innovate by powering solutions to pressing global challenges and by nurturing entrepreneurial talent to create sustainable growth and skilled jobs in Europe. The EIT is an EU body and an integral part of Horizon Europe, the EU Framework Programme for Research and Innovation.

Name: USD Women's Law Caucus
Description: The goal of the Women's Law Caucus (WLC) is to bring together the community of USD law students interested in exploring the wide spectrum of issues facing women in the legal community, as well as issues impacting women more generally. We actively accomplish this goal by 1) facilitating increased awareness and understanding of these issues through club-sponsored events; 2) empowering future female leaders and enhancing the role of women in the legal community; 3) promoting awareness of various social, economic, legal, and political issues facing all people, especially women; and 4) increasing the potential of the student body to effect meaningful change, both within the USD community and the community at large.

Name: Transform Tomorrow Today
Description:
Inspiring leaders at the forefront of transformation

In this podcast series, we invite inspiring leaders at the forefront of transformation to share their own stories. In each episode we first interview the guest and In the second part, we will distil clear how-to’s and steps for changemakers like you to apply based on the guest’s experiences and our research.

L’originalité du réseau Quartier d’Affaires est la possibilité pour les membres de découvrir tous les clubs du réseau. Et de profiter ainsi d’un relationnel dense et mobilisable.

Pour les entrepreneurs qui souhaitent s'élever en échangeant avec leurs pairs, notre réseau a mis en place des rencontres sous la forme de visites d'entreprise et de rencontres-déjeuners dans des lieux uniques qui vous permettent de développer votre entreprise à travers le partage d'expérience, la recommandation et l’entraide, vecteurs fondamentaux d'efficacité.

Pour ne pas rester isolé dans un seul club, des rencontres interclub sont régulièrement organisées et cela fait toute la différence.

A contrepied des réseaux axés uniquement sur le business, vous pouvez aller plus loin dans le partage avec des rencontres sympa et sans contraintes comme en témoigne l’engouement sur les 4 formats proposés. Et cela n'empêche pas les résultats, bien au contraire !

Quartier d’Affaires peut vous emmener là où vous voulez.

Car la différence entre un dirigeant qui réussit et ceux pour qui c’est plus difficile c’est le réseau.

Parce que se voir tous les 15 jours lors de nos plus de 800 rencontres annuelles crée des liens très forts entre nous et de ces liens nait une véritable amitié et une véritable solidarité.

J’ai hâte de vous rencontrer !

Name: Kjøtt- og fjørfebrannsjens Landsforbund
Description:
Kjøtt- og fjørfebransjens Landsforbund (KLF) er en interesse- og bransjeorganisasjon som representerer den privateide, frittstående delen av kjøtt-, egg- og fjørfebransjen i Norge.

KLF ble stiftet i 1910. Våren 2007 slo Kjøttbransjens Landsforbund og Fjørfebransjens Landsforening seg sammen til en organisasjon - KLF.

Organisasjonen har rundt  145 medlemsbedrifter fordelt på rundt 160 anlegg. Bedriftene er spredt over hele landet i rundt 100 kommuner og har om lag 4100 årsverk med en omsetning på til sammen 18,9 milliarder kroner.

Name: Los Angeles CFO Leadership Council
Description:
When most think of Los Angeles, they think of it as the hub of the global entertainment industry, but Greater Los Angeles is also a nexus of international trade, business, tourism, technology, sports, education, manufacturing, consumer products, healthcare, non-profit and much more.  As the third-largest metropolitan area in the United States, its economy measures $1+ trillion.  Los Angeles has also played host to the biggest events in the world soon including the 2026 World Cup, and the 2028 Summer Olympic Games.  Created for CFOs by CFOs, the CFO Leadership Council's Los Angeles Chapter is a thriving finance community that empowers members through innovative and impactful programs, and a robust network of professionals across a diverse range of industries just like the City of Angels and surrounding community.

Description:

We track and support alumni progress while raising awareness of the YALI and MWF initiatives.

Established by 2022 Mandela Washington Fellowship alumni, beneficiaries of the US State Department’s Mandela Washington Fellowship for Young African Leaders Initiative (YALI), MWFAN-SA unites past and future South African alumni. Our network includes leaders in Business Leadership, Civic Leadership, and Public Management, all working on impactful projects and initiatives.

Through MWFAN-SA, alumni discover peer projects and collaboration opportunities, creating meaningful programs that foster positive societal transformation in South Africa.

Our vision is to model collaborative, transformational, and impactful leadership for youth organizations in South Africa and the rest of Africa.

*Promote best security practice and standards

Name: AIN Positioning, Navigation & Timing Sub-Committee
Description:
We are excited to announce the merger of the Australian Institute of Navigation (AIN) and the International Global Navigation Satellite Systems (IGNSS) Association Incorporated, leading to the establishment of the new AIN - Positioning, Navigation & Timing (PNT) Sub-Committee, hereafter referred to as the AIN-PNT Sub-Committee.

With a distinguished history spanning 76 years, AIN is a proud member of the International Institutes of Navigation which includes, the United States Institute of Navigation and the Royal Institute of Navigation.  The IGNSS Association Incorporated have run international conferences for over 30 years tracking the latest advances in satellite positioning and attracting world class speakers and delegates. These esteemed organisations have made significant contributions to advancing Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) research, development, and practice throughout the decades.

 PNT2026, previously delivered under the esteemed banner of the IGNSS Conference Series, will now be officially hosted by AIN, organised by the  AIN PNT Sub-Committee, led by: 
• Prof. Andrew Dempster: Vice-President of AIN & Chair of the  PNT Sub-Committee 
• A/Prof. Craig Roberts: Deputy Secretary of AIN & Secretary of the  PNT Sub-Committee 
• Prof. Allison Kealy: Deputy Treasurer of AIN & Treasurer of the  PNT Sub-Committee

Name: BostonCISO
Description:
BostonCISO is the preeminent peer leadership network of New England chief information security officers. Our membership is comprised exclusively of CISOs (or equivalent executive roles) from public and private companies, government, education, healthcare and nonprofit organizations.

Our mission is to build a strong, professional community among  New England's information technology leaders, by providing a forum to share ideas, best practices and experiences relevant to CISOs.

We create opportunities for technology leaders to engage in substantive, issues-based discussion related to their industries and profession.  Members benefit by collaborating with others, building meaningful relationships, and promoting professional development through executive events that facilitate valuable interaction among senior-level experts.

MEMBERSHIP QUALIFICATION 
Members must be a New England CISO or equivalent senior technology leader for an organization with at least $100 million annual revenue.  Organizations over $1 billion qualify for a second membership and organizations over $5 billion may enroll a third member – provided the organization’s primary technology leader is a member in good standing.

ABOUT THE ORBIE AWARDS 
Established in 1998, the ORBIE Awards is the premier technology executive recognition program in the United States. This annual event honors chief information security officers who have demonstrated excellence in technology leadership.

Name: Landesapothekerkammer Baden-Württemberg
Description:
Die Landesapothekerkammer Baden-Württemberg (LAK) ist die Berufsvertretung der mehr als 13.500 Apothekerinnen und Apotheker im Land, die sowohl in öffentlichen Apotheken als auch in Krankenhäusern, in der Industrie, in der Verwaltung, in der Bundeswehr sowie in Forschung und Lehre tätig sind.

Die LAK setzt sich dafür ein, dass der Apothekerberuf als freier Heilberuf erhalten bleibt und die Arzneimittelversorgung über die Apotheken weiter optimiert wird. Als Körperschaft des öffentlichen Rechts garantiert sie eine wirksame Selbstverwaltung. Gleichzeitig übernimmt sie hoheitliche Aufgaben.

Das Grundgesetz hat weite Bereiche des Gesundheitswesens den Bundesländern zugeordnet. Diese haben über die jeweiligen Heilberufe-Kammergesetze bestimmt, welche Aufgaben in autonomer Regelungskompetenz von den Kammern wahrzunehmen sind.

Die Übertragung öffentlicher Aufgaben auf Körperschaften des öffentlichen Rechts setzt die Pflichtmitgliedschaft aller Angehörigen einer bestimmten Berufsgruppe voraus. Bei uneinheitlicher Mitgliedschaft wäre eine Selbstverwaltung nicht mehr möglich, sodass der Staat die übertragenen Bereiche des Gesundheitswesens zurücknehmen und wieder in eigener Zuständigkeit selbst verwalten müsste.

Kammern sichern ihren Mitgliedern also einen Freiraum gegenüber dem Staat, indem sie ihre Interessen in eigener Verantwortung wahrnehmen. Das erfolgt praxisnäher und wirtschaftlicher als bei staatlicher Zuständigkeit. Im Gegensatz zu Verbänden haben Kammern eine Rechtssetzungs- und Aufsichtsfunktion über ihre Mitglieder.

Name: The Academy for Professionalism in Health Care (APHC)
Description:
The overriding goal of the Academy’s work is to serve patients’ best interests by advancing accountable and assessable educational programs, policies, and methodologies that exhort current and future health care professionals to provide care consistent with the highest ethical and professional standards.

APHC will devise and implement plans to address the needs of educators in ethics and humanities, social and behavioral sciences, and clinical medicine toward the improvement of professionalism. This will include developing strategies related to educational issues of importance to the Academy, reviewing opportunities for education of members, and educating outside organizations and the public about professionalism in healthcare and APHC’s mission.

The Academy promotes scholarly inquiry regarding education in professionalism with members with diverse backgrounds. It is the expectation that APHC may from time to time issue position statements on professionalism education in healthcare that result from such scholarly inquiry.

Name: Deutsche Gesellschaft für Krisenmanagement e.V. (DGfKM)
Description:
Als Berufsverband vernetzt die Deutsche Gesellschaft für Krisenmanagement e.V. (DGfKM) seit fast zwanzig Jahren Experten für außergewöhnliche Situationen in den vier deutschsprachigen Ländern Europas. Mitglieder sind Krisen- und Compliancemanager, Risikomanagement- und Krisen-PR-Fachleute, Business Continuity Manager, Restrukturierungs- und Insolvenzspezialisten.

Die DGfKM unterstützt den Erfahrungsaustausch von Unternehmenspraktikern untereinander sowie mit der Wissenschaft. Ihre Mitglieder sind - je nach Schwerpunkt ihrer beruflichen Tätigkeit - in zwei Fachverbänden innerhalb der DGfKM organisiert: dem "blauen Club" für Krisen-, BCM-, Notfall- und Compliancemanager und dem "roten Club" für Restrukturierungs-, Risiko- und Insolvenzmanager. 

Kongresse: Die DGfKM fördert die seit 2001 mehrmals jährlich stattfindenden Gipfeltreffen der Krisen- und Restrukturierungsmanager an Universitäten im deutschsprachigen Europa sowie Fachtagungen zu krisennahen Themen.

Publikationen: Informationen aus erster Hand liefern die Fachzeitschriften "Krisenmagazin" und "Restrukturierungsmagazin" des Krisennavigator - Institut für Krisenforschung, die die DGfKM-Mitglieder exklusiv erhalten.

Fortbildung: Für seine berufsständischen Mitglieder führt die DGfKM die vier Zertifikatslehrgänge "Krisenkommunikationsmanager", "Business Continuity Manager", "Restrukturierungsmanager" und "Katastrophenmanager" durch.

Netzwerk: Ein branchenübergreifendes "Networking" und eine erstklassige berufliche Weiterbildung ermöglichen die vertraulichen Netzwerktreffen in den Mitgliedsunternehmen sowie die Fachinformationen im Intranetportal.

Weitere Informationen zur Mitgliedschaft unter www.dgfkm.de

Name: BDGW Bundesvereinigung Deutscher Geld- und Wertdienste
Description:
Die BDGW  ist Wirtschafts- und Arbeitgeberverband

Insgesamt verfügt die Branche über 2.500 gepanzerte Spezialgeldtransportfahrzeuge und beschäftigt circa 11.000 Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeiter. Davon sind 2/3 im Geld- und Werttransport und 1/3 in der Geldbearbeitung tätig. Der Umsatz der gesamten Branche beträgt auch im Jahr 2010 ca. 500 Mio. Euro. Die BDGW-Mitgliedsunternehmen verfügen über 70 Prozent Marktanteil.


Der BDGW-Sicherheitsstandard

Die BDGW hat getragen von ihren Mitgliedern einen 5-stufigen BDGW-Sicherheitsstandard entwickelt. Hierzu wurden der klassische „Sicherheits-Check 1“, der sich vor allem mit baulich-technischen Anforderungen und der Durchführung von Geld- und Werttransporten beschäftigt, auf eine völlig neue Grundlage gestellt. Dieser Sicherheits-Check 1 wurde nun um einen „Sicherheits-Check 2“ ergänzt, der sich der Regelmäßigkeit der buchhalterischen Prozesse einschließlich der Geldflüsse von Kundengeldern widmet.

Name: Confecomerç
Description:
Desde 2019, Confecomerç - Confederació d’Empresaris, Servicis i Autònoms de la Comunitat Valenciana - vela por los intereses del pequeño comercio de nuestra Comunidad y fomenta el movimiento asociativo comercial como organización empresarial del comercio minorista con mayor representatividad a nivel autonómico.

Más de 200 asociaciones locales, sectoriales y de mercados municipales de toda la Comunitat forma parte de este proyecto, que busca mejorar la competitividad de los más de 12.000 comercios a los que representamos. 
Ejercemos con responsabilidad, conscientes del peso de nuestro sector, el segundo a nivel estratégico de la Comunitat, nuestra labor de interlocución con la Administración y con otros agentes e instituciones públicas y privadas, vigilando, defendiendo y proponiendo soluciones que ayuden a mejorar la situación de nuestras pymes y autónomos y propiciando el mejor entorno y condiciones para que desarrollen su actividad. 

Y damos respuesta directa a los retos de las pymes, micropymes y autónomos de comercio de la Comunitat, que son uno de los principales motores de nuestra economía, con un 12% del PIB y un volumen de negocio superior a los 22.000 millones de € al año, a través de soluciones que pasan por el asesoramiento especializado, el ahorro de costes, la innovación y la digitalización, la formación, la sostenibilidad, la promoción del comercio y la concienciación social.

No lo hacemos solos. Formamos parte de otras organizaciones de comercio de ámbito nacional, como la CEC, y trabajamos en colaboración con otros sectores, desde organizaciones como la CEV, Cepyme o la CEOE, creando y aprovechando las sinergias, porque estamos convencidos de que crear futuro local requiere de alianzas y un enfoque multisectorial.

Sabemos que estando unidos, se hacen fuertes las asociaciones y los comercios. Y, por eso, estamos junto a las asociaciones para ayudarlas a crecer, y junto a los comercios, para que sean más competitivos.

¿Nos hacemos fuertes juntos?

Name: Slovenian Association of Clinical Pharmacy
Description:
The Slovenian Association of Clinical Pharmacy (SACP) is an independent organization that plays a significant role in promoting the development and excellence in the field of clinical pharmacy in Slovenia. Its goal is to ensure optimal drug therapy, thereby contributing to improving patients' healthcare outcomes.
 
The key missions of SACP include:

1. Promotion of Recognition and Development of Clinical Pharmacy:
SACP strives to enhance the recognition of clinical pharmacy as an indispensable part of the healthcare system. Association's dedication to advancing the profession contributes to progress in the use and management of medications and the reduction of medication-related risks.
 
2. Maintenance of the Profession's Reputation:
The organization is committed to preserving and enhancing the clinical pharmacy profession's reputation in all healthcare areas. SACP aims to promote respect and constructive collaboration with the broader community of healthcare professionals.
 
3. Advocacy for the Role of Clinical Pharmacists:
SACP advocates for the essential role of clinical pharmacists in healthcare teams at all levels of healthcare delivery. It serves as a messenger emphasizing that clinical pharmacists are fundamental and necessary contributors to all medication-related treatment processes.
 
4. Education and Training:
SACP strives to educate and raise awareness among the general public and professionals about the importance of clinical pharmacy. Additionally, in collaboration with stakeholders, it participates in defining education standards and guidelines.
 
5. Networking and Professional Community Development:
The organisation connects professionals in the field of clinical pharmacy and related disciplines. Through the organization of professional meetings, discussions, and events, SACP encourages the exchange of knowledge and experiences.

Name: EWA Catalyst Council | Women Entrepreneurs Enablement Community| Incubator/ Accelerator |
Description:
From Idea to Launch, ONE platform to meet every Entrepreneurial needs 
To fully support women founders in their entrepreneurial journey, it is essential to establish a 360-degree organization working at the grassroots level.  EWA as an organization would focus on the following areas:

1. Access to Capital: EWA would create tailored channels for women-led ventures to access funding, including grants, loans, and investment opportunities. By fostering connections with angel investors, venture capitalists, and other funding sources, EWA aims to overcome the significant barrier of securing adequate funding.

2. Mentorship and Community Building: EWA recognizes the value of mentorship and networking for women founders. It would facilitate mentorship programs, pairing successful women entrepreneurs with emerging talent. Additionally, EWA would organize networking events, workshops, and conferences to help women founders build relationships, share knowledge, and explore new business opportunities.

3. Skills Development: Equipping women founders with the necessary skills and knowledge is crucial for their success. EWA would offer workshops, training programs, and resources covering various business areas, such as leadership, finance, marketing, and technology. Strengthening their skill sets would enable women founders to confidently navigate the challenges of scaling up their ventures.

4. Legal and Financial Empowerment: EWA understands the importance of understanding legal complexities. It aims to provide tailored legal assistance and resources to women founders. EWA strives to empower women founders in finance, recognizing the critical role it plays in business growth.

By establishing a comprehensive support organization, We create an enabling environment for women to thrive as entrepreneurs.  EWA, fosters a more inclusive, innovative, and prosperous business ecosystem for everyone involved.

Name: AEDNIT - Association of Engineers of the National Department of Transportation Infrastructure.
Description:
The Association of Engineers of the National Department of Transportation Infrastructure (AEDNIT) is a non-profit association headquartered in Brasília/DF. Our mission is to defend the Technical Corps of DNIT, promote their autonomy, and contribute to the technical and cultural development of the associated engineers.

In the current scenario, transportation infrastructure plays a crucial role in the socio-economic development of a country. The National Department of Transportation Infrastructure (DNIT) is responsible for designing, constructing, maintaining, and operating the federal road infrastructure in Brazil. As engineers, we have the important task of ensuring that these roads are safe, efficient, and sustainable.

AEDNIT emerges as a unified voice for DNIT engineers, advocating for their interests and working to strengthen their presence in the sector. Our association aims to create an environment of collaboration, learning, and growth where members can connect, share knowledge, and promote professional excellence.

One of the main responsibilities of AEDNIT is the defense of the Technical Corps of DNIT. We recognize the importance of these highly skilled and committed professionals who play a vital role in the conception, planning, and execution of transportation infrastructure projects. We work to ensure that they have the appropriate working conditions, necessary resources, and professional recognition.

Description:
Welcome to the Employee Wellbeing Council

Transforming Human-Centric HR
Human-Centric HR transforms traditional human resources management by prioritizing employees' well-being, development, and empowerment. Organizations can enhance engagement, productivity, and innovation by focusing on the individual and fostering a supportive work environment.

About Us
Welcome to the Employee Wellbeing Council, where we unite thought leaders in people management, DEI, wellness, health, and safety to redefine workplace wellbeing with a commitment to real-world impact. Three core pillars shape our approach:

1 Professional Expertise: Harnessing the knowledge and skills of industry leaders.
2 Lived Experience: Valuing the unique perspectives and experiences of individuals.
3 Human-Centered Design: Creating solutions that prioritize the human aspect of the workplace.

Our Objective
The Wellbeing Council believes sustainable workplace wellbeing is achievable through a holistic approach. By leveraging the council's diverse backgrounds, we discuss emerging strategies to enhance performance and foster a culture of resilience. We aim to implement actionable insights that promote a healthier, more productive, and inclusive work environment for all.

Name: Society of Emergency Medicine Pharmacists
Description:
The Society of Emergency Medicine Pharmacists (SEMP) is the dedicated global professional home for pharmacists and allied professionals advancing acute care pharmacotherapy. 

Our Mission: To advance emergency medicine pharmacy and optimize pharmacotherapeutic care in emergency departments through practice improvement, education, advocacy, research, and international collaboration.

What We Do:
-Practice Advancement: Develop evidence-based standards and advocate for the integration of EM pharmacists to enhance patient safety and outcomes.
-Education: Provide specialized professional development and continuing education via webinars, workshops, and our flagship annual meeting, the EmpowerRx Conference.
-Research: Foster collaborative research initiatives and disseminate cutting-edge findings through our official peer-reviewed publication, the Journal of Acute Care Pharmacotherapy (JACP).
-Advocacy: Serve as a unified voice promoting the value, recognition, and optimal utilization of emergency medicine pharmacists within healthcare systems globally.
-Community: Build a supportive, inclusive international network for knowledge exchange, mentorship, and professional growth among acute care practitioners.


Join us in shaping the future of emergency medicine pharmacy worldwide. Connect with colleagues, access specialized resources, and contribute to advancing patient care in critical settings.

Name: Groupe de la Coopération au CESE
Description:
Le Groupe de la Coopération représente les coopératives françaises au Conseil Economique, Social et Environnemental (CESE), à travers 3 organisations : 
- La Coopération Agricole (lacooperationagricole.coop)
- La Confédération Générale des SCOP et SCIC (les-scop.coop)
- La Fédération Nationale des Coop HLM (hlm.coop)

Qu'est ce qu'une coopérative ? 
C'est une association autonome de personnes volontairement réunies pour satisfaire leurs aspirations et besoins économiques, sociaux, culturels communs à travers une entreprise détenue collectivement et contrôlée démocratiquement. 

Quelques chiffres pour la France :  22 600 coopératives, 1,3 millions de salariés, 350 Mds € de chiffre d'affaires. 

Comment ça fonctionne ?
- 1 personne = 1 voix
- pas d'actionnaires extérieurs
- non délocalisable
- profits redistribués équitablement aux membres ou réinjectés dans l'entreprise
- vision et stratégie de long terme

Nos valeurs : Entraide, Responsabilité, Démocratie, Egalité, Equité, Solidarité.

Der Verband setzt sich für die Förderung des Berufsstandes der Optometrist:innen gegenüber Öffentlichkeit und Behörden ein, fördert mit der Organisation von Kongressen und Kursen die Fort- und Weiterbildung seiner Mitglieder und vernetzt Fachpersonen zur gegenseitigen fachlichen Unterstützung und interdisziplinären Zusammenarbeit.

Der Berufsverband wurde 1980 durch den Zusammenschluss des Berufsverbands der Schweizer Augenoptiker mit Fachschulausbildung BSAF und dem Schweizerischen Berufsverband für Optometrie SBO gegründet. Der SBAO arbeitet partnerschaftlich mit den Branchenverbänden OPTIKSCHWEIZ, AOR und AOVS sowie dem Industrieverband OPTICS Swiss Suppliers Association zusammen.
